stories.headline: President Trump Signs H.R. 6938 into Law
stories.summary: President Donald J. Trump signed H.R. 6938, the Commerce, Justice, Science; Energy and Water Development; and Interior and Environment Appropriations Act for 2026, into law on March 31, 2026. This occurred to advance American strength by funding key agencies while rescinding Biden-era spending on DEI and energy-inefficient programs. Republican subcommittee chairmen praised it for prioritizing national priorities over wasteful initiatives.[1]

stories.headline: President Trump Signs Executive Order on March 31, 2026
stories.summary: President Trump signed an executive order on March 31, 2026, as captured in a White House video. This action aligns with his administration's pattern of using executive power to implement America First policies rapidly without congressional delay. It contributes to the 27 executive orders issued in 2026, focusing on key domestic and security issues.[7][8]

stories.headline: Trump Invokes Section 232 on Advanced Computing Chips
stories.summary: President Trump signed a proclamation under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act to address national security risks from semiconductor imports. This protects U.S. economic and military strength by incentivizing domestic production and reducing reliance on foreign supply chains, amid threats to critical tech infrastructure. It builds on prior tariffs and signals potential broader semiconductor tariffs.[3]

stories.headline: Trump Directs Negotiations on Critical Minerals Imports
stories.summary: President Trump issued a Section 232 proclamation directing negotiations with trading partners to adjust imports of processed critical minerals and derivatives due to national security threats. This promotes U.S. industry by establishing price floors and preparing for tariffs if talks fail within 180 days, following an earlier investigation into import reliance. It aims to restore American dominance in essential resources for tech and defense.[6]

stories.headline: One-Year Mark of Trump's Mass Deportation Campaign
stories.summary: One year into Trump's second term, his mass deportation efforts have impacted tens of thousands in Florida by canceling humanitarian parole and temporary protected status. This stems from his Day One America First immigration push to prioritize U.S. citizens amid housing affordability crises. State lawmakers, including Florida's DeSantis launching a government efficiency task force, are responding to related economic pressures.[2]

stories.headline: Trump Establishes America First Arms Transfer Strategy
stories.summary: President Trump signed an executive order creating the America First Arms Transfer Strategy to bolster U.S. defense industrial base. This reindustrializes America by prioritizing arms transfers that build production capacity, strengthen supply chains, and support allies aligned with U.S. security strategy. It follows an April 2025 order to speed up foreign defense sales for economic and national security.[5]

stories.headline: Meta Announces Upcoming AI Smart Glasses with Advanced Features
stories.summary: Meta revealed details on two new AI smart glasses models starting at $499, launching April 14 in the US and select markets, featuring all-day wear design, nutrition tracking via voice and photos, and WhatsApp message summarization by Meta AI. This move expands Meta's wearable AI ecosystem to target niche health-conscious users and compete with upcoming products from Google, Apple, and Snapchat. It happened amid intensifying competition in AI wearables, following pauses in prior expansions due to inventory issues.

stories.headline: Anthropic Releases Claude Mythos 5 with 10 Trillion Parameters
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Mythos 5, a 10-trillion parameter model excelling in cybersecurity, coding, and academic reasoning, alongside the more accessible Capabara model. This release advances AI capabilities for complex tasks while prioritizing broader accessibility for startups and developers. It occurred as part of April 2026's wave of model innovations driven by the race for superior performance in specialized domains.
stories.tag: NEW MODEL RELEASES · Large Language Models

stories.headline: Google DeepMind Unveils Gemini 3.1 with Multimodal Real-Time Capabilities
stories.summary: Google DeepMind introduced Gemini 3.1, supporting real-time voice and image analysis for healthcare, customer service, and autonomous systems, plus Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite for 2.5x faster responses at $0.25 per million tokens. These models emphasize efficiency and affordability, reducing memory needs and benefiting startups. The launches reflect industry pressures to lower AI costs and enable real-time applications amid economic shifts.

stories.headline: Nvidia and Marvell Announce $2B AI Alliance
stories.summary: Nvidia and Marvell formed a $2 billion partnership to advance AI infrastructure, focusing on enhanced computing capabilities. This alliance strengthens enterprise AI hardware supply chains amid surging demand. It was driven by the need for scalable AI processing power as models grow larger and more compute-intensive.

stories.headline: Oracle Cuts 20-30K Jobs to Redirect $8-10B to AI Infrastructure
stories.summary: Oracle plans to lay off 20,000 to 30,000 employees to reallocate $8-10 billion toward AI infrastructure investments. This shift prioritizes AI development over traditional operations, enabling cost reductions that benefit startups. It stems from rapid changes in AI economics, where infrastructure bets are essential for competitiveness.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Advances AI Deregulation for US Dominance
stories.summary: The Trump administration is promoting AI dominance through accelerated innovation, infrastructure, deregulation, and technology exports. This policy framework aims to outpace global competitors by reducing barriers to AI development. It emerged from strategic efforts to establish American leadership in the 'Great Divergence' of AI capabilities.

stories.headline: Q1 2026 Venture Funding Hits Record $297 Billion Driven by AI Megarounds
stories.summary: Investors poured $297 billion into 6,000 startups globally in Q1 2026, up 150% QoQ and YoY, marking an all-time high fueled by AI compute and frontier labs. Four of the five largest rounds ever—OpenAI ($120B), Anthropic ($30B), xAI ($20B), Waymo ($16B)—accounted for 64% of total funding, shattering records and pushing unicorn valuations up $900B.

stories.headline: OpenAI Closes Massive $120 Billion Round in Q1 2026
stories.summary: OpenAI raised $120 billion in one of the largest venture rounds ever, contributing to Q1's record fundraising tally. This funding underscores AI's dominance, attracting megafunders like Amazon and Nvidia while nearing $1T valuation.

stories.headline: Anthropic Secures $30 Billion Series G at $380 Billion Valuation
stories.summary: Anthropic raised $30 billion in a record-setting Series G, part of Q1's top megadeals dominating 64% of global VC. This positions Anthropic as a key player in frontier AI amid surging startup valuations and teased 2026 IPOs.

stories.headline: xAI Raises $20 Billion Series E Fueling AI Investment Boom
stories.summary: xAI closed a $20 billion Series E in Q1 2026, one of the largest rounds ever and part of AI's 64% share of global VC. Building on prior massive raises, it exemplifies the velocity of funding for top AI labs with IPO rumors.

stories.headline: Waymo Lands $16 Billion in Historic Self-Driving Funding
stories.summary: Alphabet's Waymo raised $16 billion in Q1 2026, ranking among the top five largest VC rounds ever. This deal reflects strategic bets on mobility innovation alongside the broader AI-fueled venture boom.

stories.headline: AI Seed Startups Command $40M+ Valuations in YC Cohorts
stories.summary: Y Combinator's latest cohort shows AI seed startups routinely hitting $40 million valuations, amid booming investor appetite. This signals AI's early-stage premium as record VC capital flows into the sector.

stories.headline: WTI Oil Price Prediction Markets Show $106+ at 55% Odds on April 1, 2026
stories.summary: Robinhood's prediction market indicates a 55% probability for WTI oil exceeding $106 per barrel on April 1, 2026, with $107+ at 49%. This reflects trader expectations amid geopolitical tensions and supply concerns. Markets anticipate potential price surges despite forecasts of oversupply.

stories.headline: WTI Micro April '26 Futures Trade at $96.14 with Resistance at $107.93
stories.summary: Barchart reports Crude Oil WTI Micro Apr '26 (CYJ26) last price at $96.14, facing first resistance at $100.43 and third at $107.93. Recent sessions showed volatility, with May WTI rallying to 3-week highs on supply tightness signals. Prices climbed due to broader crude advances despite daily fluctuations.

stories.headline: Goldman Sachs Raises Q4 2026 Oil Forecast to $60 Brent/$56 WTI on Low OECD Stocks
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs hiked its year-end 2026 Brent forecast to $60 and WTI to $56 per barrel, citing lower-than-expected OECD inventory levels. Despite a projected 2.3 million bpd surplus, limited stock builds prompted the upward revision. OPEC+ may resume production hikes in Q2 2026 amid shifting dynamics.

stories.headline: Trump Predicts Iran Conflict End in 2-3 Weeks, Fuel Prices to Crash Post-US Exit
stories.summary: U.S. President Trump stated the Iran war could conclude in 2-3 weeks, with U.S. forces stepping back soon, leading to a sharp drop in global fuel prices. Remarks highlight U.S. withdrawal plans independent of deals with Iran. Oil markets are reacting intensely to these Middle East geopolitical updates.

stories.headline: BOJ Member Warns High Crude Prices Risk Japan Economic Stagnation Amid Rising Costs
stories.summary: A Bank of Japan member cautioned that elevated crude oil prices could trigger economic stagnation due to escalating costs. Japan's economy shows recovery signs, but oil-driven inflation threatens growth. This comes as global oil benchmarks hover near resistance levels.

stories.headline: Oil Prices Jump on Prospects of US Military Campaign in Iran
stories.summary: Oil prices have surged recently due to fears of a U.S. military campaign in Iran, amplifying supply disruption risks. Goldman Sachs maintains no disruption base case but notes geopolitical premiums. This volatility coincides with prediction markets betting on $100+ WTI by April 1.

stories.headline: Eli Lilly Pays $6.3 Billion Upfront for Sleep Biotech
stories.summary: Eli Lilly acquired a sleep-focused biotech for $6.3 billion upfront to bolster its neuroscience portfolio amid growing demand for innovative sleep disorder treatments. This deal reflects Big Pharma's aggressive M&A strategy to secure novel assets in high-unmet-need areas. It occurred as competitors race to address insomnia and related conditions post-patent cliffs.

stories.headline: Novo Nordisk Wins FDA Approval for Awiqli, First Once-Weekly Basal Insulin
stories.summary: The FDA approved Novo Nordisk’s Awiqli for adults with type 2 diabetes, offering the first once-weekly basal insulin alternative to daily injections. This approval addresses patient adherence challenges and expands treatment options in the booming diabetes market. It stems from positive clinical data demonstrating efficacy and safety comparable to existing therapies.

stories.headline: Denali Therapeutics Gains FDA Approval for Avlayah, First New Hunter Syndrome Therapy in 20 Years
stories.summary: Denali’s Avlayah received FDA approval for Hunter Syndrome, a rare lysosomal storage disease, as the first new therapy in two decades by crossing the blood-brain barrier for whole-body treatment. This milestone advances care for a devastating pediatric condition with limited options. Approval followed robust clinical evidence of improved outcomes.

stories.headline: Novartis Acquires Excellergy for $2 Billion to Expand Allergy Portfolio
stories.summary: Novartis bought private biotech Excellergy for $2 billion, the second blockbuster acquisition in March, targeting breakthrough treatments for food allergies and IgE-driven diseases. This move strengthens Novartis' leadership in immunology amid rising allergy prevalence. It was driven by Excellergy's promising pipeline and strategic fit.

stories.headline: Merck to Acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals for $6.7 Billion
stories.summary: Merck agreed to acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals for $6.7 billion to enhance its cancer drug pipeline with Terns’ experimental HPK1 inhibitor. The deal prepares Merck for revenue pressures from the impending patent cliff on key oncology drugs. It highlights oncology as a priority for Big Pharma expansion.

stories.headline: FTC Launches Health Care Task Force for Antitrust Enforcement
stories.summary: The Federal Trade Commission created a dedicated health care task force to boost antitrust enforcement, targeting industry consolidation, pricing, and anti-competitive practices. This initiative coordinates across FTC units to increase oversight amid rising concerns over pharma mergers and market power. It responds to growing scrutiny of healthcare sector dynamics.

stories.headline: Targeting Longevity 2026 Congress Shifts Focus to Biological Coordination in Aging
stories.summary: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity, set for April 8–9, 2026 in Berlin, marks a conceptual turning point by reframing aging as a loss of coordination between systems like mitochondria, microbiota, and immunity rather than a defect to fix. This shift happened due to emerging research showing interconnected influences, such as mitochondrial signaling on senescence and microbiota-brain interactions, prioritizing system-level resilience over single interventions. Organizers from World Mitochondria Society aim to foster holistic strategies for realistic longevity breakthroughs.

stories.headline: Life Biosciences Secures FDA Approval for First Partial Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial
stories.summary: Life Biosciences, cofounded by David Sinclair, received FDA approval for a Phase 1 trial testing gene therapy to partially reset cell age and restore vision in glaucoma and NAION patients. This milestone occurred because Sinclair's research adapted Yamanaka factors to rejuvenate cells without full reset risks like tumors, enabling safer therapeutic de-aging. The approval positions the small team ahead in the billionaire-funded longevity race with a staged approach starting with optic neuropathies.

stories.headline: 2026 Ushers in Routine Biological Age Testing and MSC Therapies as Longevity Standard
stories.summary: 2026 is declared the year longevity becomes standard care, with biological age tests as routine as physicals and advanced mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) therapies bridging lifespan and healthspan by combating chronic inflammation. This evolution stems from clinical breakthroughs, like a 2024 study showing MSC secretome repairs radiation-damaged brain cells and promotes new capillaries, shifting from symptom management to proactive aging control. Concepts like 'bio-liquidity' emerge for preserving young cells for lifelong regeneration.

stories.headline: Organ-on-a-Chip and AI-Driven Drug Design Set to Accelerate Senolytics and Longevity Therapies
stories.summary: By 2026, organ-on-a-chip technologies enable real-time testing of longevity drugs like senolytics on human-like tissues, bypassing unreliable mouse data. This advancement arises from integrating AI to design drugs, test on chips and digital twins, and fast-track to trials, exploring vastly more chemical space to boost odds of extending healthspan. Pathways targeting fibrosis and inflammation have already entered Phase 1/2, redefining drug discovery efficiency.

stories.headline: CD38 Inhibitors Show Lifespan Extension in Mice, Poised for Human Aging Trials
stories.summary: Small molecule inhibitors targeting CD38 correct aging-related issues and extend lifespan in mice, as presented by Eric Verdin in a March 2026 talk. Development progressed because CD38 drives metabolic and immune aging hallmarks, and these drugs comprehensively address them better than prior approaches. Plans include testing such interventions on human intrinsic capacity markers within three years.

stories.headline: Epigenetic Clocks and Rapamycin Highlighted as Top Longevity Trends for 2026
stories.summary: Epigenetic clocks, analyzing DNA methylation for biological age, predict healthspan and disease risk, while rapamycin modulates mTOR to curb cellular aging and inflammation. These trends gain prominence due to their proven links to aging pathways—clocks via reliable biomarkers and rapamycin's FDA-approved history from Easter Island discovery—fueling personalized, high-tech longevity strategies. Integration with wearables and recovery tools like HRV optimization amplifies whole-person health approaches.

stories.headline: NASA Artemis II Launch Set for April 1 with 80% Favorable Weather
stories.summary: NASA is targeting a 6:24 p.m. ET launch window on April 1 for Artemis II, the first crewed lunar mission since 1972, sending four astronauts on a 10-day orbit around the Moon aboard the SLS rocket and Orion spacecraft. This followed repairs for a helium leak discovered during February tests, which delayed the mission from March and prompted a rollback to the Vehicle Assembly Building. Favorable weather and completed readiness reviews make April 1 the prime opportunity within the window ending April 6.

stories.headline: All Systems Go for Artemis II Liftoff After L-1 Briefing
stories.summary: NASA's L-1 countdown briefing on March 31 confirmed all preparations on track for the April 1 Artemis II launch at 6:24 p.m. EDT, with only a 20% weather violation risk. Delays from a helium issue in the SLS upper stage were resolved after rolling the rocket back for fixes, enabling the mission to proceed as the first crewed test of the SLS-Orion system. The launch supports NASA's Artemis program goals for lunar exploration and Mars preparation.

stories.headline: Artemis II Rocket Rolled Back to Pad After Helium Repairs
stories.summary: The SLS rocket for Artemis II was rolled to Launch Complex 39B after completing repairs for a helium flow issue identified in late February, clearing the path for the April 1 launch. This fix addressed problems from a prior fueling test, pushing the schedule from early March to ensure crew safety on the historic lunar flyby. NASA conducted a Flight Readiness Review on March 12, approving the rollout and final configuration.

stories.headline: X1.4 Solar Flare Prompts Space Weather Monitoring for Artemis II
stories.summary: A powerful X1.4 solar flare on March 29 caused a strong radio blackout, leading NASA to monitor space weather closely ahead of the April 1 Artemis II launch. Such events could disrupt communications or preparations, but current conditions remain favorable. This vigilance ensures the safety of the four-astronaut crew during their 10-day lunar mission.

stories.headline: NASA L-1 Status Conference Confirms April 1 Artemis II Readiness
stories.summary: NASA's March 31 L-1 news conference with key leaders like test director Jeff Spaulding affirmed final preparations for the Artemis II launch on April 1 from Kennedy Space Center. The two-hour window opens at 6:24 p.m. EDT, following resolution of technical issues to enable the crewed lunar orbit. This mission tests systems for future Moon landings and Mars voyages under the Artemis program.

stories.headline: Artemis II: First Crewed Moon Mission Since Apollo Targets April 1
stories.summary: NASA aims for an April 1 launch of Artemis II, the farthest human spaceflight ever, orbiting the Moon without landing to validate Orion for deeper space. Delays from fuel leaks and helium issues during tests necessitated repairs, shifting from February to this date with backups through April 6. The mission advances U.S. lunar return goals amid infrastructure pushes.

stories.headline: Broadcasters Urge FCC to Block Live Sports Paywalls on Streaming Services
stories.summary: Broadcasters including Fox Corp. and Sinclair expressed concerns to the FCC on March 30, 2026, arguing that leagues are abusing the 1961 Sports Broadcasting Act's antitrust exemption to shift games to subscription-based streaming platforms. This move sidelines free over-the-air access, driven by streamers' deep pockets outbidding traditional TV. The complaints highlight rising government scrutiny amid fragmented sports viewing and public subsidies for stadiums.

stories.headline: Skillz Releases Q4 2025 Earnings on March 31, Reports $30M Revenue Amid Losses
stories.summary: Skillz Inc. announced its Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results after market close on March 31, 2026, posting $30 million revenue, $26.5 million gross profit, but a $17.9 million net loss and $10 million adjusted EBITDA loss. The competitive mobile gaming platform saw 141,000 paying monthly active users, reflecting ongoing investments in growth despite profitability challenges in a maturing market. A conference call followed on April 1 to discuss strategies.

stories.headline: WNBA CBA Talks Escalate with Senior Players Threatening 2025-2026 Opt-Out
stories.summary: Senior WNBA players on March 12, 2025, framed upcoming CBA negotiations as a potential work-stoppage point, elevating 2025–2026 talks to system-level debates on revenue sharing, salary bands, travel standards, and expansion. This stems from the post-House v. NCAA settlement transforming athlete compensation and governance. It raises business-continuity risks for teams and sponsors entering multi-year deals.

stories.headline: Dave & Buster's Schedules Q4 and FY 2025 Earnings Release for March 31
stories.summary: Dave & Buster's Entertainment Inc. announced it will report Q4 and fiscal year-end 2025 financial results on March 31, 2026, amid ongoing recovery in experiential entertainment. The release reflects investor interest in arcade and dining venue performance post-pandemic. Details to be shared via GlobeNewswire.

stories.headline: AI Reshapes Global Sports Industry Operations and Convergence with Entertainment
stories.summary: Deloitte's 2026 outlook highlights AI as a foundational growth layer integrating business silos, while capital inflows evolve ownership and blur sports-media boundaries. Media rights values climb worldwide, women's sports revenues grow double-digits, and stadiums become year-round districts, driven by global fan expansion and resilient revenues. Sports emerges as a broader entertainment frontier.

stories.headline: Private Equity Surges into Sports Franchises as Entertainment Platforms
stories.summary: LA Times reports strong private equity and global investor interest in sports franchises due to diversified revenues from media rights, sponsorships, and events. Teams in L.A. operate as entertainment platforms, boosted by NIL enabling early athlete branding and marketing. This strengthens valuations amid league stability and global fan growth.
